manifest.name: Notekeeper

name: Notekeeper

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
Visual polish across both light and dark themes. No API changes; should not affect the bot review.

## Light/dark adaptivity

- New **"Theme (auto)" wall background** that uses Obsidian's `--background-primary` and `--background-modifier-border` directly. **Now the default for new installs** — adapts cleanly to whatever theme you're running.
- Light-theme overrides for the dark-only walls (Slate, Blueprint, Chalkboard, Cork). Picking one of those in light mode no longer slams a dark wall into a light card surface.
- Single light-theme block at the bottom of `styles.css` covering cards, tag chips, hover-action buttons, the active filter chip, sort + archive + density toolbar, capture action row, bulk-action floating bar, and modal action buttons on uncolored notes.

## Colored-surface text/icon contrast

- Cards with a color get a `has-card-color` class. Title, body, headings, links, code blocks, blockquotes, tag chips, meta, action icons, and skeleton shimmer all switch to white/light against the dark color palette.
- **Hover-action buttons on colored cards** now have an explicit dark translucent backdrop instead of floating white icons.
- The capture wrap gets the same treatment when a color is chosen — title, body, placeholders, action row, and Discard button all flip to light.
- The note-preview modal inherits the note's color and applies the same readability rules.

## Capture box

- **Title field** above the body, both visible only when capture is focused
- **Pin** and **Color** toggles in the action row
- **Discard** button next to a primary **Save** button
- **Auto-save on focus loss** (200ms grace so the color picker popup doesn't trigger a premature save)
- Plain Enter inserts a newline; ⌘+Enter saves explicitly

## Wall view

- Pinned cards float to the top in pinOrder
- 3D pushpin head on pinned wall cards
- Capture in wall mode auto-adds the new note to the current wall
- `Clear wall` command (palette) wipes the current wall
- Removed the floating + and the wall switcher dropdown — one implicit wall, capture is the way to add

## Lint

- `Discard (Esc)` → `Discard (esc)` (sentence-case)
- `document.activeElement` → `activeDocument.activeElement` (popout-window compat)
- Local lint: 0 errors, 0 warnings against the obsidianmd recommended config + `require-await`
releases.name: 0.2.2 — Theme polish

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
## Capture box (Keep parity)

- Title input above body, visible only when capture is focused
- **Discard** button next to a primary **Save** button
- Pin and color toggles in the action row; picking a color tints the whole capture so you preview the sticky color before saving
- Auto-save on focus loss (200 ms grace window so the color popup doesn't trigger a premature save)
- Plain Enter inserts a newline; ⌘/Ctrl + Enter saves
- Title typography tuned: 20 px / 500 weight, divider between title and body, no nested input borders
- Capture also auto-adds the new note to the current wall when in wall mode

## Wall view

- Pinned cards float to the top in pinOrder
- 3D pushpin head on pinned wall cards (red gradient + drop shadow, softer shadow on light backgrounds)
- Removed the floating `+` add button (redundant — the capture box auto-adds in wall mode)
- Removed the wall switcher dropdown; one implicit wall, plus a **Clear wall** command in the palette

## Modal preview

- Inherits the note's color: paints the modal background, transparent inner panels, lighter tag chips and code-block backgrounds for contrast on the dark color palette
- Hid Obsidian's built-in `×` close (it overlapped the action row); Esc / click-outside / Open-in-tab still close the modal

## Bulk actions

- 40 px buttons with 20 px icons (was 32 / 16) for better legibility
- Vertical divider between selection count and action buttons

## Internals

- `onOpen`, `onClose`, `render`, and the bulk-trash handler no longer declare `async` without an `await`; `require-await` passes locally
- Switched view content lookup from `containerEl.children[1]` to `contentEl` (Obsidian docs recommendation; fixes a tab-switching ghosting bug)
releases.name: 0.2.1 — Capture redesign + colored modal

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
## ✨ New: Wall mode

A sticky-note styled, curated view of your notes. Two new density modes alongside the existing three:

- **Wall** — masonry layout with sticky-note styling: deterministic 1-3 degree rotation seeded per file, paper-style drop shadow, and one of eight backgrounds (cork, whiteboard, slate, paper, graph paper, blueprint, linen, chalkboard).
- **Wall (compact)** — same look, smaller cards / more columns at the same width.

Walls are **curated**, not auto-filled. The wall starts empty; add notes via the floating **+** button (fuzzy picker) or the empty state's *"Fill from current view"* shortcut (offered when the current filter has ≤ 200 notes). Right-click any card on the wall for **"Remove from wall"**. Use the *"Clear wall"* command (⌘P) to wipe the wall and start over.

**Wall is now the default density** for new installs. Background choice lives in **Settings → Notekeeper → Wall background**, defaulting to Slate.

## 🐛 Fixes flagged by the community-store reviewer bot

- `onOpen`, `onClose`, `render`, and the bulk-delete arrow are no longer marked `async` without awaits — `require-await` now passes.
- Switched the view content lookup from `this.containerEl.children[1]` to `this.contentEl`. This fixes a tab-switching bug where the wall view's UI would ghost into other tabs — newer Obsidian versions can wrap a view in extra DOM layers, which shifted the children index and made my render land on the wrong element. `contentEl` is the stable getter recommended by the docs.
- The wall's floating "+" button moved from `position: fixed` to `position: absolute` inside the body, so it disappears with its tab instead of staying painted across panes.

## ⚙️ Other

- `@typescript-eslint/require-await` added to the local eslint config so this category doesn't slip past again.
releases.name: 0.2.0 — Wall mode

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
## Reviewer-bot fixes

- Bumps minAppVersion to 1.7.2 (lowest version covering every API the plugin touches — `processFrontMatter` and `revealLeaf` set the floor)
- Drops the deprecated `MarkdownRenderer.renderMarkdown` fallback in favor of `MarkdownRenderer.render`
- Replaces `Workspace.activeLeaf` reads with `Workspace.getActiveViewOfType`
- Replaces `Vault.trash()` with `FileManager.trashFile()` so deletion respects the user's configured preference
- Replaces `window.confirm()` with a custom `ConfirmModal` for the bulk-delete prompt
- Dynamic style assignments now use `HTMLElement.setCssStyles` instead of writing to `element.style.*` directly
- All static styling in the tag-management modals has been moved out of inline styles and into CSS classes
- Async event handlers that returned `Promise<void>` where the listener expects `void` are now sync wrappers around `void await`
- Empty `catch` blocks now have explanatory comments
- Removes `this` aliasing in `openNotePreview` (uses an arrow function instead)
- Drops unused vars and fixes sentence case in setting descriptions

ESLint with `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` is now clean: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

## Source

`main.ts` is the canonical source. Build with `npm install && npm run build`. `main.js` is committed alongside so manual installs still work without a build step.

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
## Fixes

- **Pin, archive, and color no longer touch the note file.** Previously `processFrontMatter` was used to write these to YAML frontmatter, which (a) reformatted users' frontmatter (comments stripped, flow lists converted to block, key order normalized) and (b) bumped `stat.mtime`, causing cards to jump to the top of the recent-sorted grid every time they were toggled. All three now live in the plugin's own `data.json` keyed by file path.
- Frontmatter `pinned:` / `archived:` / `color:` are still **read** as a fallback so notes the user authored manually with these keys keep working unchanged.
- Storing an explicit `false` (pin/archive) or `null` (color) in plugin data lets the UI override a frontmatter value when the user toggles off via the picker.
- Rename and delete events migrate or drop the entries automatically.

## Docs

- README and the upcoming Dev.to post no longer claim that `processFrontMatter` preserves frontmatter formatting (it doesn't).

Thanks to the reviewer in the obsidian-releases PR for catching both issues.

releases.author: PhilemonChiro
releases.description:
Initial release of Notekeeper.

A Google Keep-style masonry card view for Obsidian.

- Browse notes as a masonry of cards, sized to content
- Quick-capture, inline edit, and a markdown-rendered preview modal
- Pin, color, archive; 12-color palette; drag pinned cards to reorder
- Multi-select with bulk pin / archive / color / delete
- Right-click context menu, inline checkboxes, image thumbnails, backlinks badge
- Smart search: tag:, pinned:, archived:, color:, path:, is:
- Sort by modified, created, or title
- Three view densities: comfortable, compact, list
- Lazy infinite scroll
- Auto-collapse Obsidian's side panes while the card view is active
- Manage labels command: rename a tag across the vault, assign per-tag colors

## Manual install

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below
2. Copy them into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/notekeeper/`
3. Reload Obsidian and enable Notekeeper in Settings → Community plugins
